title: leptosiphon ciliatus
intro: Leptosiphon ciliatus, also known as the hairy maire or woolly maire, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and colds due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing. Leptosiphon ciliatus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for consumption, with some traditional practitioners also using it in topical applications for skin conditions and injuries.
